L1.description: Use organizational theory to develop strategies to implement changes with a shared vision consistent with the midwifery model of care and aligned and engaged with relevant stakeholders.

L2.description: Anticipate and prepare for the future by maintaining awareness of the internal and external environments affecting midwifery.

L3.description: Build and lead teams, empowering team members to work independently and together toward a common goal.

L4.description: Lead with integrity and confidence stemming from an awareness of one’s own strengths, weaknesses, and biases.

L5.description: Collaborate with other health care leaders in the development of systems that improve the standard of care for persons seeking midwifery care.

L6.description: Secure access to resources in concert with community and stakeholders and take action when available resources are not used efficiently.

L7.description: Develop the capacity of others to lead, creating an environment in which diversity in leadership will thrive.

L8.description: Critique and improve structures that perpetuate harm and employ innovative strategies that promote well-being and equity for organizational members and the communities they serve.

PH1.description: Apply epidemiological tenets to identify the distribution (frequency and pattern) of health-related outcomes.

PH2.description: Apply epidemiological tenets to describe the determinants of health-related states and events.

PH3.description: Use public health principles to promote health as defined by people and their communities, focus on promoting healthy lifestyles, address social and structural determinants of health, and respond to health disparities.

PH4.description: Use public health principles to promote health care equity, quality, and accessibility.

PH5.description: Promote health globally by applying midwifery skills and knowledge to global health systems, governments, agencies, and organizations to optimize health.

PH6.description: Promote health globally by developing, in coordination with communities, organizations, and governments, best practices in the delivery of health care at diverse geographic levels (eg, municipalities, states/provinces, countries).

PH7.description: Promote health globally by responding to global health threats, including outbreaks of biologic diseases, environmental hazards, and chronic disease threats that disregard national borders.

PH8.description: Promote health globally by advancing evidence-based models of reproductive and sexual health care.

PH9.description: Promote health globally by applying principles of respect, cultural humility, co-development, sustainability, and self-determination to minimize harm and foster equitable relationships with global partners.

HS1.description: Lead, support, or advise institutions or complex systems on quality care initiatives to improve individual, community, and health care system outcomes.

HS2.description: Integrate theories, models, and best practices into health care settings to improve the health of people, including midwives and their colleagues.

HS3.description: Use macroeconomic and microeconomic principles to guide the development of health system and supporting organization programs.

HS4.description: Use macroeconomic and microeconomic principles to guide advocacy work in support of midwives and their patients.

HS5.description: Assess the cost-effectiveness of models of care in clinical practice.

PA1.description: Analyze the impact of legislation, judicial opinions, regulations, and policies on reproductive, family, and community health.

PA2.description: Apply an antiracist lens to policy making and advocacy.

PA3.description: Build coalitions designed to influence and implement policy initiatives in concert with communities and stakeholders.

PA4.description: Serve as an expert advisor to policy makers on health issues, law, and policy.

PA5.description: Influence health policy and program decision-making based on scientific evidence, health equity principles, and ethical precepts.

PA6.description: Develop, promote, and implement policies that support physiologic processes and discourage intervention in the absence of complications.

PA7.description: Create and advocate for policies that support rights to self-determination and informed choice for people seeking midwifery care.

PA8.description: Present scientific evidence to advance sexual, reproductive, and primary health care policy in an engaging and persuasive manner.

DA1.description: Interpret and critically appraise existing literature and other evidence not only for direct applicability to practice, but also to inform the need for further study.

DA2.description: Use appropriate theory and methodologies to develop, implement, and evaluate projects to improve the health care of individuals and families seeking midwifery care.

DA3.description: Evaluate the use of existing databases and provide leadership in the development of new data sets to address phenomena of interest to the practice and profession of midwifery.

DA4.description: Translate expert clinical knowledge and research relevant to midwifery into best practice, education, and advocacy models.

IT1.description: Appraise and use information and technology to design, implement, and evaluate programs and practices which improve quality in and access to midwifery care.

IT2.description: Use information technology in the collaborative generation of policy, clinical knowledge, and education innovation.

IT3.description: Use a clinical perspective to evaluate information systems for accessibility, relevance, cost-effectiveness, and efficacy.

IT4.description: Provide leadership and accountability regarding ethical, racial, and social justice and legal aspects of information and technology related to midwifery.

SD1.description: Complete projects relevant to the profession of midwifery that demonstrate alignment with the Hallmarks of Midwifery.

SD2.description: Complete projects relevant to the profession of midwifery that demonstrate integration and interpretation of relevant literature.

SD3.description: Complete projects relevant to the profession of midwifery that demonstrate intersection with political, social, or economic policies and programs that will improve health in diverse populations.

SD4.description: Complete projects relevant to the profession of midwifery that demonstrate an ability to design a policy, program, project, strategic plan, or intervention.

SD5.description: Complete projects relevant to the profession of midwifery that demonstrate strategies to identify stakeholders and build coalitions and partnerships.

SD6.description: Complete projects relevant to the profession of midwifery that demonstrate analysis, interpretation, and display of data.

SD7.description: Complete projects relevant to the profession of midwifery that demonstrate communication and dissemination of audience-appropriate midwifery scholarship in writing, visually, and in oral presentations.

SD8.description: Complete projects relevant to the profession of midwifery that demonstrate expertise and shared knowledge in a selected area of midwifery practice or scholarship beyond the ACNM Core Competencies for Basic Midwifery Practice.

SD9.description: Produce written documents that synthesize evidence and disseminate knowledge.

SD10.description: Prepare and submit scholarly manuscripts for publication.
